title: Web WIZARDD Intake Form Schema
description: Captures everything the A.R.C. cannot provide — aesthetic preferences, brand assets, design archetype direction, page selection, CTA preferences, differentiators, and out-of-scope flags. Completed by the Allegiant team member initiating the build during or after the partner onboarding conversation.

partner.description: Partner identification. businessName must match the A.R.C.'s businessIdentity.businessName exactly — the tool cross-validates and blocks if they diverge.

businessName.description: Must exactly match the A.R.C. businessIdentity.businessName. Catches the 'wrong A.R.C. linked to wrong intake' failure mode.

salesforcePartnerId.description: Optional Salesforce or CRM ID for downstream system linking.

brandAssets.description: Partner brand assets. Each section has an 'available' boolean — if false, Web WIZARDD composes a recommendation from the design archetype defaults and flags it for partner approval.

needsCreation.description: True if partner has no logo and needs one created. Triggers an out-of-scope notice (logo creation is not a Web WIZARDD deliverable; flag for separate engagement).

stamp.description: Stamp/icon variant of the logo if available.

colorTokens.description: Partner brand color palette. If unavailable, Web WIZARDD recommends a palette from the design archetype defaults tuned to the vertical.

neutral.description: Neutral ramp for backgrounds, borders, text.

semantic.description: Semantic state colors (success, warning, error, info). Generated from accent if not provided.

photographyStyle.description: Partner's existing photography library and style direction.

libraryUrl.description: Google Drive, Dropbox, or other shared library URL.

styleNotes.description: Free-text style direction. Used to inform image prompt generation when AI-generated images are produced.
examples: Documentary-style on-the-job shots, warm and authentic, no stock-looking imagery

approach.description: How images for the site will be sourced.

designDirection.description: Three archetypes are presented to the partner per build; the selected archetype family lives here. The actual three-direction generation happens in the Generation layer based on this family.

archetypeFamily.description: The archetype family for this vertical. For home services: trust-first, authority, velocity. Additional families are added as new verticals are encountered.

rationale.description: Why this archetype family was selected for this partner. Captured at intake; reviewed at design selection.

verticalAdjustments.description: Vertical-specific notes that modify the archetype defaults.

selectedDirectionAtSignoff.description: Populated after partner review. Identifies which of the three generated directions the partner selected. Not required at intake.

pageSelection.description: Which pages are in scope for the v1 mockup phase. Home page is always included. v1 produces home + one interior service page across three design directions = six total mockups.

homePage.description: Always true in v1. Reserved field for future configurability.

serviceName.description: Must exactly match one of the names in the A.R.C. targetServices array. Cross-validated by the tool.

rationale.description: Why this service was chosen for the v1 mockup (usually: highest LTV, primary persona anchor, biggest competitive gap).

additionalRequestedPages.description: Pages the partner specifically requested be represented in the v1 mockup phase beyond home and the selected service page. Out-of-default scope — flag to the team that these add lift.

ctaPreferences.description: Call-to-action framing confirmed with the partner. Ties to the messaging pillars from the A.R.C. audience.decisionDrivers.

phoneNumber.description: Click-to-call number. Used in sticky mobile CTAs and click-to-call schema.

differentiators.description: Partner's stated edges that should appear above-the-fold on the home page. Should be substantive and verifiable (not generic claims). Audited at Gauntlet P4 by the Lawyer reader.

outOfScope.description: Things the partner has explicitly asked not to address on the site. Hard constraints — Generation must not produce content touching these.

complianceFlags.description: Vertical-specific compliance requirements that affect content generation. Examples: legal disclaimers for legal/medical, HIPAA for medical, FTC for affiliate or financial content.

operator.description: Bookkeeping fields. Builder identity drives Gauntlet peer-review assignment and override approval logging.

builderName.description: Allegiant team member initiating the build. Must be an active @allegiantdigital.com identity.

intakeFormVersion.description: Schema version this intake was completed against. Helps the tool gracefully handle older intakes if the schema evolves.

buildId.description: Optional pre-assigned build ID. If not provided, Web WIZARDD generates one.
